title: Compose Specification, Services, Projects, Dependencies, and Lifecycle · Operations Transfer
summary: An independent delivery team receives unfamiliar application constraints and must transfer compose specification, services, projects, dependencies, and lifecycle into a new validated multi-service Compose application model without copying the workshop scenario. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of validated multi-service Compose application model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Explain the current Compose Specification and service, network, volume, config, secret, project, and optional implementation boundaries.
Resolve project name, working directory, files, includes, merges, interpolation, environment sources, and canonical config output.
Define image or build source, command, entrypoint, environment, user, working directory, health, resources, and metadata per service.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Compose Spec Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab three-service project has hidden interpolation, name collisions, start-order assumptions, a mutable image, and teardown that may delete intake data. Render the model, dry-run lifecycle, and prove preservation.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Compose Spec Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain the current compose specification and service, network, volume, config, secret, project, and optional implementation boundaries. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Compose Spec Model, keep Docker Compose Spec Model connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: A lab three-service project has hidden interpolation, name collisions, start-order assumptions, a mutable image, and teardown that may delete intake data. Render the model, dry-run lifecycle, and prove preservation.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Docker Compose Spec Model condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Compose Specification and Docker Compose 5.3.1 — Controls services, projects, interpolation, merges, builds, dependencies, health, networks, volumes, configs, secrets, profiles, development, and lifecycle.

This does not satisfy Docker Compose Spec Model for validated multi-service Compose application model.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-compose-up-down-state-qnu5u2
  - competency: docker-compose-up-down-state
    artifact: compose.yaml
    decision: compose up down state
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: multi-tenant build farm using an isolated BuildKit driver
    fault-injection: deny outbound DNS after initial service discovery
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: qnu5u2
    change: |
      services:
      api:
      image: registry.example.test/intake@sha256:verified
      depends_on:
      db:
      condition: service_healthy
      volumes:
      intake-data:
    verify: docker compose config --quiet && docker compose --dry-run up --build
    failure: Reject start order as readiness proof, implicit project identity, floating images, or teardown that silently deletes required volumes.
    repair: Render the merged model, pin identities, encode health dependencies, inspect the dry run, and document down versus down --volumes.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The Docker Compose Up Down State structural check is only one gate. validated multi-service Compose application model must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Predict pull, build, create, recreate, start, attach, stop, down, orphan, anonymous volume, and named volume effects before lifecycle commands.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Compose Up Down State, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Compose Up Down State precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Compose Up Down State, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A lab three-service project has hidden interpolation, name collisions, start-order assumptions, a mutable image, and teardown that may delete intake data. Render the model, dry-run lifecycle, and prove preservation. Competency probe: Predict pull, build, create, recreate, start, attach, stop, down, orphan, anonymous volume, and named volume effects before lifecycle commands.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Compose Up Down State, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: docker compose down removes every named volume and built image by default.
